name: Snooky Young
abstract: Eugene Edward "Snooky" Young (February 3, 1919 – May 11, 2011) was an American jazz trumpeter. He was known for his mastery of the plunger mute, with which he was able to create a wide range of sounds.

interviews.title: Snooky Young and Gerald Wilson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Count Basie and His Orchestra
Newport Jazz Festival: Count Basie and His Orchestra
Carnegie Hall Jazz Band: Jimmie Lunceford and Tommy Dorsey: Then and Now
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: I played with Snooky Young. ... Snooky needed an alto saxophonist. I think he asked me if I wanted to play with him. I said, “Yes,” ... They had this seven-week engagement in Detroit, which began in August of 1949. I was to go perform with him
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Shew
about_quotes.evidence: Snooky Young stood next to me on Louie Bellson’s band for a long time and he was also, we were in Bill Berry’s band together and also in the Juggernaut, Nat Pierce/Frank Capp band together.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Shew interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 1, 2020

about_quotes.speaker: Gerald Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: Yes, Snooky plays in my LA band... Snooky, who had been with Count Basie’s band – he quit Count Basie’s band to join my band!
about_quotes.doc_title: Gerald Wilson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: JJ Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: We became very good friends through the years and we still are... He was really a good buddy of mine
about_quotes.doc_title: JJ Johnson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Clay Jenkins
about_quotes.evidence: Snooky and I were together in the Clayton-Hamilton band for 26 years.
about_quotes.doc_title: Clay Jenkins interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 18, 2016

about_quotes.speaker: Gene Bertoncini
about_quotes.evidence: And then my years on the Tonight Show in New York for a couple of years I had to learn — and that trumpet section with Doc Severinsen as the lead trumpet player, Clark Terry and Snooky Young and Jimmy Maxwell.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gene Bertoncini interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001
